mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals strengthening consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS monthly change versus 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the direction of correlation is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) drops below 5.0%, the labor market becomes increasingly tight, raising wage inflation expectations and prompting anticipatory Fed tightening, which depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE below 5.0% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicate a hawkish Fed policy stance that raises discount rates and squeezes equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise month-over-month, the median 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive, or the Rank IC of the joint rise signal is below 0.03 over three years.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment defined by FEDFUNDS below 2.0%, rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong real economic activity and improving corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 2%, the correlation between INDPRO month-over-month changes and 20-day forward returns is negative, or the signal fails to produce a positive median return in more than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) increase exceeding 150,000 indicates robust job creation and rising household income, which boosts consumption and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In events where PAYEMS exceeds 150,000, the average 5-day forward S&P 500 return is negative or not statistically different from zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding output and capacity utilization, which supports higher corporate earnings and economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between INDPRO monthly percentage change and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns remains below 0.03 over any consecutive 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals broad-based consumer and residential demand, which drives GDP growth and corporate profitability, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where both HOUST and RSAFS increase sequentially, the median 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative, or the joint signal has a hit rate below 50% across all expansionary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year below 2.0%), an increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals Fed confidence in the economic outlook and a normalization of policy that reduces uncertainty, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY is below 2%, the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day forward returns is negative, or the median return in such periods is not positive.
